description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, blocky stone elements, forming a robust and imposing structure. The archway is centrally positioned, flanked by thick piers composed of stacked rectangular stones, and topped with a series of flat capstones. The keystone at the apex of the arch is prominent, supporting the weight of the upper stones. The base features several protruding stones, possibly remnants of a plinth or foundation, and a circular stone element lies adjacent to one side. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative reliefs or inscriptions, evoking the monumental style of ancient Roman architecture.
description: A massive stone triumphal arch with a central arched passage, thick blocky piers, and a simple, undecorated design reminiscent of Roman monuments.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full arched opening, thick stone piers, and a circular stone element on the right.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the depth of the arch and the stacked stone construction, with the circular stone visible in front.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, showing the archway and blocky construction, but the circular stone is now on the left.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again highlights the depth and layered stones, with the circular stone element behind the arch.
